How Should You Install Software on a Mac? Homebrew vs Official Download vs curl

If you've just started using a Mac for development, you've probably noticed there are several different ways to install the same tool — download a `.dmg` from the official site, install with Homebrew, run a `curl` install script, and tutorials sometimes recommend different approaches for the same tool. This post sorts through the common Mac install methods, pairs them with real examples (Git, Node.js, Python, VS Code), so you know which one to pick when.
Homebrew zsh: command not found: brew — How I Fixed It

One day brew install just stopped working — zsh told me it couldn't find "brew install", but plain `brew` did show output. This post walks through what was wrong (PATH-related setup against the wrong shell rc file, plus a missing wget dependency), and the steps that actually got brew install back up and running.
Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (3): Installing It
Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle
第 13 屆 iT邦幫忙鐵人賽

Next up is actually installing Python — because if we want to learn Python, we kind of need Python on our machine first. The install looks trivial, but Windows and macOS each have their own quirks and a couple of gotchas, so I'm giving both of them their own walkthrough here.
Homebrew: homebrew-core / homebrew-cask Is a Shallow Clone — How to Fix It

Recently when installing Homebrew, I started seeing "homebrew-core is a shallow clone." and "homebrew-cask is a shallow clone." errors during install. This post records the fix — two git fetch --unshallow commands you need to run manually to make the install complete.
